title: Bowtie Risk Table
description: A structured risk assessment following the Bowtie methodology. Models hazards with their top events, threats (causes), consequences (effects), and barriers (controls) at each level.

id.description: Unique identifier for the risk table.

hazards.description: Top-level hazards identified in the assessment.

Hazard.description: A hazard — the central element of a Bowtie diagram. Represents a source of potential harm with an associated top event.

topEventName.description: The top event — the moment the hazard is realised (e.g. 'Loss of containment').

topEventDescription.description: Detailed description of the top event.

threats.description: Threats (causes) that could trigger the top event. Shown on the left side of the Bowtie.

consequences.description: Consequences (effects) that result from the top event. Shown on the right side of the Bowtie.

Threat.description: A threat — a cause or scenario that could lead to the top event occurring.

description.description: Detailed description of the threat scenario.

barriers.description: Preventive barriers — controls that prevent this threat from causing the top event.

Consequence.description: A consequence — an outcome that results from the top event occurring.

barriers.description: Mitigative barriers — controls that reduce the impact after the top event occurs.

Barrier.description: A barrier — a control measure that either prevents the top event (preventive) or reduces the consequence (mitigative).

description.description: How this barrier works and what it controls.

effectiveness.description: Estimated effectiveness of the barrier (0.0 = ineffective, 1.0 = fully effective).
